mechanism_narrative: A sustained multi-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) signals deteriorating residential investment and household balance-sheet stress that precedes broader consumption and credit contraction, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1467k units, down from 2005-06 peak above 2000k
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and 60-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX posts positive returns in >55% of months with declining HOUST trend.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains above a restrictive threshold of 5.0% for multiple months, tight financing conditions compress corporate margins and raise the equity discount rate, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days until the Fed begins cutting.
failure_condition: 20-day forward SPX returns are not significantly lower (t-test p>0.05) than baseline during periods with FEDFUNDS>5.0% for 3+ months, or hypothesis fails to reject null across 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A joint pattern of decelerating payroll growth (PAYEMS) combined with a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals an inflection from labor-market strength to weakness, and this dual confirmation predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days more reliably than either series alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth falls below 1% AND UNRATE rises month-over-month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6%, historically near cycle lows
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes show forward 20-day SPX returns statistically indistinguishable from unconditional distribution (p>0.10), or joint signal underperforms single-series PAYEMS-only signal in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where housing starts are falling while the Fed funds rate remains elevated, the combination of credit tightening and housing-sector contraction amplifies downside risk to consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter that are stronger than in a housing downturn alone.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns during joint HOUST-decline/high-FEDFUNDS regime are not significantly more negative than during HOUST-decline-only periods (p>0.05), invalidating the interaction premise.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side momentum that supports near-term corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns show no significant positive correlation (Rank IC<0.02) with INDPRO month-over-month growth across a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales (RSAFS) exceeding a meaningful threshold signals weakening consumer spending, which comprises the majority of GDP, and predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as growth forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: Instances where RSAFS falls >0.5% MoM are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in >60% of cases, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the nominal Fed funds rate exceeds year-over-year CPI inflation by a wide margin, the resulting high real interest rate tightens financial conditions and compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter; this effect is only detectable through the joint behavior of both series, not either alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS 5.26% vs CPI index 208.028 implying moderate real rate
failure_condition: Real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPI YoY) shows Rank IC below 0.03 against 60-day forward SPX returns over any 3-year rolling window, or effect is not distinguishable from FEDFUNDS-only signal.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate of 0.3 percentage points or more from its 12-month low acts as an early recession-warning threshold (Sahm-rule-like signal) that predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in higher recession risk.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >= 0.3pp above its trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.6%, near cycle low, no trigger yet activated
failure_condition: In historical instances where UNRATE rises >=0.3pp from trailing low, 20-day forward SPX returns are positive in >55% of cases, or signal shows no significant lead over baseline in walk-forward testing.
